name: Trier
description: Experimental consumer actively exploring the brand and category
context: Trier is in an active exploration phase—they've recently purchased the brand and are evaluating whether to make it a regular choice. They're comparing across multiple brands, reading reviews, trying different products, and forming opinions. This is a critical window where positive experiences can build habit and negative ones will eliminate the brand from consideration. They're information-seeking and experience-focused.

psychographics.decisionMakingStyle: Deliberate and comparative. Actively gathering information. Open to persuasion but skeptical of marketing.

alwaysAsks: How does this compare to alternatives?
What makes this worth trying again?
Is this the right fit for me?
Is this better than what I've tried before?
evaluationCriteria.reactsToEarlyIdeas: Evaluates as an active learner. Wants education, comparison clarity, and trial incentives to continue exploring.
evaluationCriteria.scoringRubric: Scores on differentiation clarity, trial-to-repeat value, education quality, comparison advantages, and exploration support.

exampleQuotes: I'm trying a bunch of different brands right now
It was good, but I want to see what else is out there
Can you explain how this is different from [competitor]?
I'm still figuring out what I like
I'll probably try it one more time before I decide
keyInsights: Triers are in the most persuadable moment—active consideration phase
First impressions and early experiences are disproportionately important
They need reasons to repeat, not just reasons to try once
Education and comparison clarity can accelerate decision-making
Many triers will revert to old habits without strong positive reinforcement
